api/spec/packages/aip/src/currencies/cos.purpose: Defines TypeSpec models and REST operations for billing cost bases — per-fiat-currency rate records attached to custom currencies. All output is compiled via `make gen-api`; no hand-written OpenAPI or Go here.

patterns.name: Visibility-scoped fields
patterns.description: Every field must declare @visibility(Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Read) or @visibility(Lifecycle.Read). System-assigned fields (id, created_at) are Read-only. Omitting @visibility leaks system fields into create payloads.

patterns.name: Shared generic wrappers
patterns.description: Use Shared.CreateRequest<T> / Shared.CreateResponse<T> / Shared.PagePaginatedResponse<T> for all request/response envelopes — never write ad-hoc body or response shapes.

patterns.name: deepObject filter query params
patterns.description: Filter parameters must be grouped into a dedicated filter model annotated @query(#{ style: 'deepObject', explode: true }) so URL params follow filter[field]=value convention.

patterns.name: Extension decorators for stability
patterns.description: Every operation must carry @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true) and @extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true). Missing these breaks internal API maturity tracking.

patterns.name: Explicit @operationId and @summary
patterns.description: Every operation must declare @operationId (kebab-case) and @summary for deterministic SDK method naming and OpenAPI documentation.

patterns.name: Namespace scoping
patterns.description: All models and operations must be declared inside `namespace Currencies;` matching the parent domain. Never use the root namespace or a different one.

patterns.name: Pagination via Common.PagePaginationQuery spread
patterns.description: List operations spread ...Common.PagePaginationQuery into the parameter list rather than declaring page/pageSize manually.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the CostBasis model — single source of truth for field names, types, and visibility. @friendlyName sets the generated Go/JS type name.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Omitting @visibility causes fields to appear in both create and read payloads. @friendlyName must be globally unique across the spec — duplicates cause SDK type collisions.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the CurrenciesCustomCostBasesOperations interface with list and create endpoints. All imports, HTTP decorators, and using statements live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing 'using TypeSpec.Http;' makes @get/@post/@query unknown decorators. Error union must include both domain-specific errors (Common.NotFound) and the catch-all Common.ErrorResponses.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing generated OpenAPI or Go files instead of modifying these .tsp sources and running make gen-api
Declaring fields without @visibility — fields default to all lifecycle phases and leak write-only or system fields into create payloads
Adding operations without the stability @extension decorators — breaks internal API maturity tracking
Writing ad-hoc pagination parameters instead of spreading ...Common.PagePaginationQuery
Using a @friendlyName that duplicates an existing model name — causes SDK type collisions

decisions.decision: Models and operations split across two files (cost-basis.tsp + operations.tsp)
decisions.rationale: Keeps the domain model (fields, types) separate from HTTP routing concerns (decorators, error unions, query params), matching the pattern used by sibling folders under aip/src/.
Cost-basis endpoints are billing-internal and not yet stable; the extensions keep them out of public SDK docs and signal breaking-change freedom.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new read-only field to CostBasis (e.g. updated_at)

api/spec/packages/aip/src/customers/char.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the customer charges API — the Charge discriminated union (flat_fee, usage_based), shared ChargeBase<T> generic model, charge enums, and the list operation with filter/sort/expand. Compiles into v3 OpenAPI and SDKs; types live in charges.tsp, HTTP operations in operations.tsp.

patterns.name: ChargeBase<T> spread for concrete models
patterns.description: All concrete charge models (FlatFeeCharge, UsageBasedCharge) spread ChargeBase<ChargeType.X> to inherit shared read-only fields. Type-specific fields are added only on the concrete model, never on ChargeBase.

patterns.name: Discriminated union with envelope:none
patterns.description: Charge is a union with @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }). Variant keys match enum values verbatim. New charge types require adding to both ChargeType enum and the union.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Charge { flat_fee: FlatFeeCharge, usage_based: UsageBasedCharge }

patterns.name: @visibility Read for response-only, Create for writable fields
patterns.description: All ChargeBase fields are @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) — server-assigned. Fields settable on creation use @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create). No Update visibility — charges have no PATCH operation.

patterns.name: @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") on all models and enums
patterns.description: Every exported model and enum must carry @friendlyName("Billing<PascalCase>") so the generated SDK name is stable and prefixed with Billing.

patterns.name: HTTP decorators belong only in operations.tsp
patterns.description: charges.tsp holds types only. operations.tsp imports @typespec/http, declares using statements, and owns @get/@path/@query/@operationId usage.

patterns.name: deepObject explode for filter query params
patterns.description: Filter query params use @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) to support bracket-style filters like filter[status][oeq]=created.

patterns.name: index.tsp re-exports all sibling files
patterns.description: Every .tsp file in this folder must be imported in index.tsp in dependency order or it is invisible to the compiler.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all charge models (ChargeBase<T>, FlatFeeCharge, UsageBasedCharge, ChargeTotals), enums (ChargeType, ChargeStatus), the Charge discriminated union, and ChargesExpand enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add HTTP decorators here. Concrete models must spread ChargeBase<T> — do not duplicate shared fields. Do not use Lifecycle.Update.

key_file_guides.role: Declares CustomerChargesOperations interface with the list operation, filter/sort/expand params, and PagePaginatedResponse return type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and declare `using TypeSpec.Http;`. Response must use Shared.PagePaginatedResponse<Charge>, not a raw array. @path customerId is required.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports charges.tsp then operations.tsp in order; consumed by parent package index.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files must be added here in dependency order or the compiler will not see them.

anti_patterns: Adding HTTP decorators (@get, @post, @path, @query) in charges.tsp — they belong only in operations.tsp
Defining type-specific fields on ChargeBase instead of on FlatFeeCharge / UsageBasedCharge
Omitting @friendlyName on new models or enums — the generated SDK name will be unstable
Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Update) — charges have no PATCH operation
Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml instead of running make gen-api

decisions.decision: Discriminated union with envelope:none and discriminatorPropertyName:type
decisions.rationale: Keeps the wire format flat (no wrapper object) while letting the OpenAPI discriminator route deserialization to the correct concrete model.
Avoids duplicating the large set of shared read-only fields across FlatFeeCharge and UsageBasedCharge while keeping the type parameter visible in the generated schema.

decisions.decision: No update operation and no Lifecycle.Update visibility
decisions.rationale: Charges are managed by the billing engine lifecycle; callers cannot freely PATCH them. Mirrors the Go charges.Service interface which has no general Update method.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new charge type (e.g. credit_purchase) — changes in charges.tsp only

api/spec/packages/aip/src/customers/cred.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the customer credits API: credit grants (allocation, funding methods, lifecycle), credit balances (per-currency aggregates), credit transactions (immutable ledger entries), and credit adjustments. Compiles into v3 OpenAPI; the corresponding Go handlers are gated behind credits.enabled in api/v3/server.

patterns.name: Shared.Resource for mutable entities, Shared.ResourceImmutable for ledger entries
patterns.description: CreditGrant spreads Shared.Resource (mutable). CreditTransaction spreads Shared.ResourceImmutable to signal no updates. Balance models do not spread Resource — they are computed aggregates.
patterns.example: model CreditGrant { ...Shared.Resource; ... } model CreditTransaction { ...Shared.ResourceImmutable; ... } model CreditBalances { retrieved_at: Shared.DateTime; balances: CreditBalance[]; }

patterns.name: @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") on all exported models and enums
patterns.description: Every exported symbol carries @friendlyName("Billing<PascalCase>") for stable SDK names. Exception: CreditBalance (non-billing-prefixed) is intentional for the inner model.

patterns.name: Separate interfaces per resource in operations.tsp
patterns.description: Operations split into fine-grained interfaces: CustomerCreditGrantsOperations, CustomerCreditGrantExternalSettlementOperations, CustomerCreditBalancesOperations, CustomerCreditTransactionOperations, CustomerCreditAdjustmentsOperations. No omnibus interface.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination for transactions, page pagination for grants/adjustments
patterns.description: CreditTransaction list uses ...Common.CursorPaginationQuery + Shared.CursorPaginatedResponse<T>. CreditGrant list uses ...Common.PagePaginationQuery + Shared.PagePaginatedResponse<T>.

patterns.name: @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create) only — no Update
patterns.description: No field uses Lifecycle.Update because credits have no general PATCH; grants have only targeted action endpoints (updateExternalSettlement). Computed fields use Lifecycle.Read alone.

patterns.name: Nested anonymous object literals for compound fields
patterns.description: Tightly related sub-fields use inline anonymous objects (purchase, invoice, filters, available_balance) instead of named models, unless the sub-type needs to be referenced standalone.

patterns.name: Commented-out stubs for unimplemented features
patterns.description: Future fields (effective_at, expires_after, expires_at, OnAuthorization/OnSettlement variants) and entire interfaces (void-grant) are fully commented out. Do not uncomment without a matching Go implementation.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CreditGrant model and CreditFundingMethod, CreditAvailabilityPolicy, CreditGrantStatus, CreditGrantTaxConfig, CreditPurchasePaymentSettlementStatus enums.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Several commented-out fields are intentional stubs — do not uncomment without a matching Go charges.Service implementation. `purchase` is an anonymous nested object, not a named model.

key_file_guides.role: Declares all CRUD and action interfaces for grants, balance, transactions, and adjustments. Imports all sub-type files directly.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http. The void-grant interface is entirely commented out — do not expose until Go implements void lifecycle. balance.tsp and adjustment.tsp are imported here, not in index.tsp.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CreditBalances (list of per-currency CreditBalance) returned by the balance GET endpoint.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Does not spread Shared.Resource — it is a computed read-only aggregate, not a stored entity. Do not add id/created_at.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CreditTransaction (immutable ledger entry spreading Shared.ResourceImmutable) and CreditTransactionType enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditTransaction spreads Shared.ResourceImmutable — do not add any mutable or Create-visible fields. Actor model is fully commented out for future use.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CreditAdjustment for manual balance corrections using PickProperties<Shared.Resource> for selective field inheritance.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only Create-visible fields are present; no Read-only state fields. Currency is @visibility(Lifecycle.Create) only.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports grant.tsp, ledger.tsp, and operations.tsp. balance.tsp and adjustment.tsp are imported directly in operations.tsp, not here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New type files must be imported either in index.tsp or directly in operations.tsp before they are visible to the compiler.

anti_patterns: Uncommenting void-grant or external-payment-initial-settlement-status stubs without a matching Go charges.Service implementation
Adding @visibility(Lifecycle.Update) — no general update operation exists for any credit resource
Spreading Shared.Resource into balance.tsp models — CreditBalances is a computed aggregate, not a stored entity
Using page pagination for credit transactions — they must use cursor pagination
Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml instead of running make gen-api

decisions.decision: Cursor pagination for credit transactions, page pagination for grants
decisions.rationale: Transactions are append-only and high-volume; cursor pagination is stable under concurrent inserts. Grants are low-cardinality and need offset-based navigation for admin UIs.

decisions.decision: Separate operations interfaces per resource sub-type
decisions.rationale: Matches the Go side where each resource maps to a distinct service method group, and makes it easy to enable/disable individual endpoint groups (e.g. void-grant fully commented out until implemented).

decisions.decision: Credits API compiled in TypeSpec but gated at Go wiring layer, not at TypeSpec level
decisions.rationale: The spec is always compiled; credits.enabled disables Go handler registration in api/v3/server and noop-wires ledger services in app/common. Keeps the spec complete while the feature is in progress.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new filter field to credit grant listing — only operations.tsp changes
